summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Payment Date Bug Fix (Iteration 5). All backend tests passed (7/7). Frontend verification confirmed the bug fix is working correctly - payments before a landed entry date no longer affect that entry's interest_start_date. Chronological ledger generation with correct running balance is also verified.
passed_tests: New test account creation with bug fix verification - Entry 2 interest_start_date = its own date (2026-03-15), NOT payment date
Chronological ledger generation - entries sorted by date with correct running balance
Payment distribution - interest paid first, then principal (FIFO)
Core flows - login, account list, account detail with enriched data
Frontend Account Detail Page - Landed Entries tab shows 'Interest Start From' and 'Days' columns
Frontend Ledger tab shows chronological entries with correct running balance

bug_fix_verification.scenario: Account ACC000025 with Landed Entry 1 (2026-03-01, ₹10,000) and Landed Entry 2 (2026-03-10, ₹2,000), Received Entry (2026-03-06, ₹2,000)
bug_fix_verification.expected_behavior: Entry 1 interest_start_date = 2026-03-06 (payment date), Entry 2 interest_start_date = 2026-03-10 (its own date)
bug_fix_verification.actual_behavior: Entry 1 interest_start_date = 06 Mar 2026 ✅, Entry 2 interest_start_date = 10 Mar 2026 ✅

seed_data_creation: Test accounts created and cleaned up during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Payment date bug fix has been verified. The _entry_existed_at_payment() helper function correctly skips entries whose date > payment_date when processing payments. Chronological ledger generation via generate_chronological_ledger() is working correctly. Account ACC000025 (id: 69b820918f6a1fd1231b5224) contains the test data for this bug fix.
